name: Trentino-South Tyrol (Italy)

colors.symbolism: the field, hosting the regional coat of arms
the narrow bordering stripes and the eagle of Trento
the eagle of Tyrol on the regional emblem
the gold detailing of both eagles on the emblem

description: A white field bordered above and below by narrow red horizontal stripes, with the regional coat of arms (the impaled eagles of Trento and Tyrol within a green wreath) at the centre.
history: Defined by the regional Statute (Constitutional Law n. 5 of 31 January 1972) and subsequent regulations. The combined arms unite the eagles of the two autonomous provinces of Trento and South Tyrol (Bolzano), reflecting the bilingual region's composition.

features.symbolism: A predominantly white field bordered above and below by narrow red bars, with the regional coat of arms at the centre.

features.role: regional coat of arms (a parted-per-pale shield with the silver-and-red eagle of Trento on the dexter and the red-and-gold eagle of Tyrol on the sinister, surmounted by a mural crown and surrounded by a green laurel wreath)
features.symbolism: Combines the historic eagles of the two autonomous provinces that constitute the region: the silver eagle of the Prince-Bishopric of Trento and the red eagle of the County of Tyrol.
